# @generated by `cargo xtask gen-std-signatures` from
# `crates/trust-std/src/lib.rs`. Do not edit by hand — run the xtask
# subcommand after modifying the std-shim source. CI verifies this file is
# fresh via `cargo xtask gen-std-signatures --check`.
#
# Format: one line per public `fn` in `trust-std`. Each line is
#
#     simple_fn_name:param1,param2,...
#
# `simple_fn_name` is the unqualified function name; the parameter list is
# its declared positional order. Functions whose simple names collide
# across modules with mismatched signatures are dropped at generation time
# (the lowering pass disambiguates by simple name only). Zero-arity fns
# appear with an empty tail: `channel:`.
#
# This file is the source of truth that `trust-lower/build.rs` reads
# to seed `STD_SIGNATURES`. The previous design parsed `lib.rs` with
# `syn` at build time, but that meant `trust-std` could never itself
# be `#![strict]`-marked (syn rejects named-arg syntax). The checked-in
# manifest removes that coupling — `lib.rs` can use any Trust feature
# the xtask knows how to lower. See RT-44.

arc_new:value
btreemap_new:
channel:
command:program
command_arg:cmd,arg
command_env:cmd,key,value
copy:from,to
create_dir_all:path
duration:secs,nanos
exit:code
hashmap_insert:map,key,value
hashmap_new:
hashmap_with_capacity:capacity
hashset_new:
insert:map,key,value
millis:value
mutex_new:value
read_to_string:path
remove_file:path
rename:from,to
rwlock_new:value
set_var:name,value
sleep:duration
string_new:
string_with_capacity:capacity
tcp_connect:addr
tcp_listener_bind:addr
udp_socket_bind:addr
var:name
vec_new:
vec_push:v,value
vec_with_capacity:capacity
write_bytes:path,bytes
write_text:path,contents
